rag_rules: Fix unresolved references in AGREE contracts by ensuring all identifiers are visible in the current scope or properly declared as helpers.
Validate that AGREE expressions have valid types; resolve 'named thing must be an expression with a type' errors by correcting root reference issues.
generic_rules: When generating AGREE contracts, ensure all referenced identifiers are declared or visible in the current annex scope.
Use only scalar types int, real, and bool in AGREE const and eq declarations to avoid resolution errors.
Align helper declaration types with the types of their assigned expressions to prevent assumed vs actual type mismatches.
Avoid introducing package-qualified identifiers in AGREE code that are not present in the model's configured dependencies.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be placed in valid annex scopes: component types for assumptions/guarantees, implementations for bindings.
AGREE expressions must resolve to valid types; use only declared helpers or visible AADL features in expressions.
anti_patterns: Do not add new architecture elements to resolve AGREE reference errors; instead, correct or remove invalid clauses.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Inspector diagnostics about property associations
AGREE validator diagnostics about component type annexes
Model fusion and owner/scope preservation rules
